draft.title: weather app for runners
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
weather app for runners

Building your first draft…

facade.hypothesis: Runners prefer clean, high-contrast typography that's fast to scan in bright sunlight and during motion
facade.label: Crisp sans-serif
facade.acceptImplies: Lean into modern, geometric letterforms; prioritize legibility over warmth; use negative space efficiently
facade.rejectImplies: Explore serif or rounded fonts; warmer, more humanistic typeface treatments; softer visual texture

facade.hypothesis: A weather app for runners benefits from an encouraging, upbeat personality that pushes users to get outside—rather than neutral, data-only reporting
facade.label: Motivational voice
facade.acceptImplies: Lean into conversational tone, celebratory language, maybe emojis or exclamation marks. The app cheers the runner on.
facade.rejectImplies: Stick with clean, minimal, factual tone. No personality. Let the data speak for itself.

evidence.hypothesis: Runners prefer clean, high-contrast typography that's fast to scan in bright sunlight and during motion
evidence.implication: Lean into modern, geometric letterforms; prioritize legibility over warmth; use negative space efficiently

facade.hypothesis: Runners want spatial data (current location, route path, weather zones) shown as an interactive map rather than summarized metrics
facade.axisTargeted: Information Design: spatial visualization vs. numeric summary
facade.label: Live map with routes
facade.acceptImplies: Prioritize map-based interface, show weather conditions geographically, integrate route planning visually
facade.rejectImplies: Focus on stripped-down metrics view (pace, temp, humidity as numbers/cards), keep spatial data minimal or text-based

facade.hypothesis: A weather app for runners benefits from warm, inviting colors and soft lighting effects that feel encouraging and energetic rather than clinical or cold.
facade.label: Warm glow
facade.acceptImplies: User prefers approachable, optimistic visual language—likely favors amber/orange/warm tones, soft shadows, and a motivational rather than utilitarian aesthetic.
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers cooler, more neutral or clinical design—likely favors blues/grays, sharp contrast, and efficiency-focused presentation over emotional warmth.

facade.hypothesis: Runners need persistent, at-a-glance metrics (pace, heart rate, distance) visible while scrolling through detailed forecasts
facade.axisTargeted: Navigation structure and information density trade-off: persistent sidebar vs full-width scrollable content
facade.label: Sidebar with live stats
facade.acceptImplies: User values quick metric glances over maximum screen real estate; information hierarchy favors quantitative data prominence; app should reserve left/right edge for always-visible runner KPIs
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers uncluttered, full-width content layout; metrics should appear inline or on-demand rather than persistently; navigation and content should occupy same space fluidly

facade.hypothesis: Runners want real-time visual feedback that mirrors their heartbeat or cadence rather than static numbers
facade.axisTargeted: Motion and behavior — animated feedback vs static readout
facade.label: Pace pulses on screen
facade.acceptImplies: Build rhythm-synced UI elements (pulsing metrics, breathing animations, tempo-linked transitions)
facade.rejectImplies: Keep data presentation static and instant; prioritize clarity and quick glance-ability over emotional connection to motion

axes.label: Whether user prefers minimalist, clean aesthetics vs. data-rich visual displays for workout tracking
Whether user wants quick-glance info at top vs. scrollable detailed forecast layers
Whether user responds to motivational, encouraging tone vs. neutral, factual reporting
Whether user wants calm, focused atmosphere vs. energetic, action-oriented feel
Whether user prioritizes running-specific metrics (pace impact, humidity) vs. standard weather only
Whether user prefers smooth, gradual transitions vs. snappy, instant updates and alerts

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers minimalist, clean aesthetics vs. data-rich visual displays for workout tracking
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants quick-glance info at top vs. scrollable detailed forecast layers
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user responds to motivational, encouraging tone vs. neutral, factual reporting
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants calm, focused atmosphere vs. energetic, action-oriented feel
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prioritizes running-specific metrics (pace impact, humidity) vs. standard weather only
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers smooth, gradual transitions vs. snappy, instant updates and alerts
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: A weather app for runners benefits from subtle, dynamic visual feedback that creates a sense of immediacy and aliveness—reinforcing the connection between app interaction and physical urgency of outdoor conditions
facade.axisTargeted: organic vs digital; calm vs energetic
facade.label: Real-time glow
facade.acceptImplies: Lean into animated weather transitions, glowing accent colors that shift with conditions, micro-interactions that feel responsive and alive rather than static
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers stable, non-animated interfaces; prioritize steady visual hierarchy and restful aesthetic over dynamic energy cues

draft.title: RunWeather
draft.summary: A weather app designed for runners, showing real-time conditions, wind impact, and run-friendly forecasts at a glance.
draft.html:
RunWeather

San Francisco, CA
62 °F
Partly Cloudy
Wind
8 mph
Humidity
65%
Feels Like
58°F
UV Index
3

Hourly Forecast

Now
62°
2 PM
64°
4 PM
61°
6 PM
58°

Good for Running

Mild temps, light wind. Perfect for a 5K.
